name: of Galadriel
desc:
A small crystal phial containing the light of Eärendil's Star.
Its light is imperishable, and near it darkness cannot endure.

name: of Elendil
desc: The shining Star of the North, a treasured heirloom of Elendil's house.

name: of Thráin
desc:
A great globe seemingly filled with moonlight, the famed Heart of the
Mountain.  It splinters the light that falls upon it into ten thousand
sparks of white radiance shot with glints of the rainbow.

name: 'Angrist'
desc:
Forged by Telchar, greatest of Dwarven smiths, and used by Beren to gouge
a Silmaril out of Morgoth's crown, this long chopping dagger slices
through ordinary metal as easily as its name, "Iron Cleaver", suggests.

name: 'Narthanc'
desc: Instead of a blade, a flame seems to emerge from this dagger's hilt.

name: 'Nimthanc'
desc: From the hilt of this dagger springs not so much a blade as an icicle.

name: 'Dethanc'
desc: In this dagger is bound the very force of lightning.

name: of Rilia
desc:
A large stiletto dagger that glistens with odorless poison, to which the
wearer seems oddly immune.

name: 'Belangil'
desc:
A frosty dagger wreathed in a nimbus of ice with a hilt of elk horn and
an edge to wound the wind.

name: of Azaghâl
desc:
Azaghâl, Dwarf-king of Belegost, fell before Glaurung in the Battle
of Unnumbered Tears.  With his final breath, Azaghâl plunged this
blade into Glaurung's belly with such a blow that the Father of Dragons
fled the field with all his kin, and was at first believed to be mortally
wounded.

name: of Maedhros
desc:
A short thrusting blade with a large guard worn by the eldest son of
Fëanor, Maedhros the Tall, who lost his right hand while escaping
from Angband.

name: 'Forasgil'
desc: A slender, tapered needle of ice that coldly grips its impaled prey.

name: 'Careth Asdriag'
desc:
An heirloom of the dauntless Lords of Rhûn far to the east, and a name
of dismay to creatures natural and unnatural.

name: 'Sting'
desc:
"I will give you a name, and I shall call you Sting."  The perfect size
for Bilbo, and stamped forever by the courage he found in Mirkwood, this
sturdy little blade grants the wearer combat prowess and survival
abilities he did not know he had.

name: 'Dagmor'
desc:
A stubby blade worn by Beren, whose horn sounded of old in the glades of
Brethil.

name: 'Gondricam'
desc:
Famed sea-defender of Lebennin.  A short, slightly curved chopping blade
with a perfect edge shining cleanly in the sun, an object of hate to the
men of Umbar who met it in combat.

name: 'Haradekket'
desc:
A damascened scimitar that hefts with wondrous ease.  Famed in song
as the "Sickle of Harad", and a deadly foe to the undead.

name: 'Ringil'
desc:
The weapon of Fingolfin, High King of the Noldor; it shines like a column
of ice lit by light unquenchable.  Morgoth came but unwillingly to meet it
of old; his lame foot will remind him of its might should he face it
again.

name: 'Andúril'
desc:
The famed "Flame of the West", the Sword that was Broken and is forged
again.  It glows with the essence of fire, its wearer is mighty in combat,
and no creature of Sauron can withstand it.

name: 'Anguirel'
desc:
This black blade was forged of meteoric iron by Eöl the Dark Elf.  It is
imbued with the malice of its maker; as dangerous to its wielder as it is
deadly to his foes.

name: 'Elvagil'
desc:
The "Singing Blade", whose wearer can slay Orcs and Trolls in the hidden
and secret places of the earth.

name: 'Aranrúth'
desc:
The beautiful sword of Thingol, justly named "King's Ire".  It glistens
icy enough to freeze the hearts of demons, and you feel supple and
lightfooted as you clasp its hilt of gold and silver inlay.

name: 'Glamdring'
desc:
This fiery, shining blade, mate to Orcrist, earned its sobriquet "Beater"
from dying orcs who dared to behold hidden Gondolin.

name: 'Aeglin'
desc:
Companion to Orcrist and Glamdring, and like them long lost, this sword is
continually covered in tiny arcs of captive lightning that flash and dance
eerily in the globes of light they create.

name: 'Orcrist'
desc:
This coldly gleaming blade, mate to Glamdring, is called simply "Biter"
by orcs who came to know its power all too well.

name: 'Calris'
desc:
This sword has runes of power incused on its ornate hilt, and a single
blood channel that gleams coldly blue as you grasp this mighty weapon of
peril.

name: of Éowyn
desc:
The blade of the legendary Shieldmaiden of Rohan, who slew the foul
steed of the Morgul-lord before striking down the mighty Witch-king
himself, whom no mortal man could hinder.

name: 'Aglarang'
desc:
An utterly perfect, cleanly chiseled sword, with an edge that effortlessly
slices rock and bone, and spells to render the wearer lithe and nimble.
It is combat incarnate.

name: 'Gurthang'
desc:
A giant sword once wielded by mighty Túrin, and an even greater
dragonbane; for what worm could stand against the blade that bathed in
Glaurung's blood?

name: 'Zarcuthra'
desc:
Runes dark and deadly stand stark against the naked steel of this awesome
weapon, and you feel a wrathful power of slaying and rending as you
slowly approach.

name: 'Mormegil'
desc:
A foul, twisted sword with blackened spines and knobs, its very name is
a curse upon the lips of Elves and Men.  It is said that it will drink the
very lifeblood of its wielder.

name: 'Crisdurian'
desc:
A giant's weapon, with a long blade tall and straight thrusting out from a
massive double-pronged hilt.  On its blade are written doomspells against
both the living and undead.

name: 'Doomcaller'
desc:
This weapon of wrath, wrought by a desperate berserker gang, dives hungrily
into the yielding flesh of your enemies.  You yourself fall under the
shadow of death even as you inflict wounds that will not heal.

name: of Gothmog
desc:
A great crackling Balrog's whip of flame, this weapon could bring
even Ungoliant to heel.

name: of Fundin Bluecloak
desc:
The weapon of a mighty dwarven priest, whose shade is rumoured to
haunt the halls of Angband still.

name: 'Bloodspike'
desc:
You feel strong and firm of foot as you whip its spiked orb around and
bathe it in the blood of your foes.

name: 'Firestar'
desc:
A famed battle-lord of old with a head as ruddy as embers that can yet rise
up in wrath.

name: 'Totila'
desc:
A flail whose head befuddles those who stare as you whirl it round and
becomes a fiery comet as you bring it down.

name: 'Thunderfist'
desc:
The long-lost weapon of Kzurin, Dwarven champion of ancient Belegost
Runes of strength adorn its handle, and flames and sparks roar and
crackle around its massive head.

name: 'Taratol'
desc:
A great ridged mace that surrounds you with a nimbus of living lightning.
You remain utterly untouched, even as fat sparks crackle around your
fingers and eyebrows.

name: 'Nar-i-vagil'
desc:
Named for a fiery star and set with priceless gems bearing mystic
auras of protection and thought.

name: 'Eriril'
desc:
The radiant golden staff of an Istar of legend, this wizard's companion
grants keen sight and clear knowledge of many hidden things.

name: of Olórin
desc:
A staff tall and sturdy, borne of old by Gandalf.  Rough-hewn runes that
invoke the element of Earth stand bold upon its gnarled surface, and no
creature who lives in the shadow of mountains can withstand the blows it
deals.

name: of Aulë
desc:
The wondrous hammer of Aulë, creator of the wise Dwarven lords of old.
It bears demolishing magics that no serpent or demon can withstand, and
invokes the strength of mountains to ward off the tumult of the elements.

name: 'Deathwreaker'
desc:
A weapon so massive it seems beyond the strength of mortals, yet you feel
the might of giants within you as you heft it.  As you grip the handle
of ebony and steel, coronas of fire blaze, and mighty spells to preserve
magic surge around you.  You wield the Fear of Dragons and the Despair
of the Undead!

name: 'Aiglos'
desc:
The mighty spear of Gil-galad, famed as "Snow-point" in the songs of Elves.
Against it, all the foul corruptions of Sauron dashed in vain.

name: of Oromë
desc:
The thrusting spear of wise Oromë the Vala, strong against giants of
frost, and able to pierce rock or flesh with ease.

name: 'Nimloth'
desc:
A thin spike of thrice-forged steel caps a straight silvan shaft cut from
a legendary tree, and spells to break the wills of the undead and strike
cold into the breasts of your enemies lay upon this perfectly balanced
spear.

name: of Melkor
desc:
A mighty, heavy black spear, once wielded by the Lord of Darkness
himself as he came to Valinor in wrath.  Its envenomed bite pierced
the Two Trees with wounds that would not heal.

name: of Wrath
desc:
A massive triple-pronged spear, so great it normally requires two hands
to wield, evoking the spirit of Osse who with it pierced legions of
evil and undead.

name: of Ulmo
desc:
The awesome weapon of the Vala Ulmo, Lord of Waters.  Mightiest of all the
powers of good save Manwë himself, Ulmo laughs to scorn the dread powers
of the undead, and is utterly in command of the Element of water.

name: 'Osondir'
desc:
Lordly and tall did Osondir stand against the wrath of giants, and
clear-eyed in barrows fell, wielding a halberd glowing ruby red.

name: 'Til-i-arc'
desc:
Within this long two-handed spear dwell the spirits of frost giants and
fire demons, who war forever, fettered by sorcerous binds.

name: of Théoden
desc:
The narrow axe head of this weapon would pierce the armour of a very
Dragon, and the designs of your enemies stand naked and revealed.

name: of Húrin
desc:
Wielded by Húrin Thalion, last lord of Dor-Lómin at the Battle
of Tears Unnumbered.  He stood alone, his friends and kinsmen
dead about him, his axe smoking in the black blood of Gothmog's
troll-guard.  No less than seventy times, as he slew his foes,
did he utter his mighty war-cry: "Aurë entuluva!" - "Day shall
come again!"

name: 'Barukkheled'
desc:
A royal heirloom of the southern coast, strong in combat against the evil
creatures of earth.

name: of Balli Stonehand
desc:
The twin blades of this weapon were forged in Belegost, and powerful
forces to resist and endure lie ready for its next owner.

name: 'Lotharang'
desc:
A superbly crafted double-bladed axe once borne by Ceruthain, commander
of the Rangers of Arnor.

name: 'Mundwine'
desc:
A massive axe with twin razor-sharp heads, so large that it usually
requires two hands to wield, with spells to ward off the elements
intricately engraved upon its surface in filleted gold.

name: of Durin
desc:
The twin massive axe heads of this ancient demon's dread gleam with
mithril inlay telling sagas of endurance.  The powers of
Khazad-dûm protect its wielder and slay all evils found underground.

name: of Eönwë
desc:
The axe of Eönwë, leader of the Hosts of the West in the War of
Wrath, strikes with icy wrath at the undead, disperses hosts of
evil at a word, and grants Maia-like powers of body and mind.

name: 'Avavir'
desc:
Holding elemental powers whose struggles turn this weapon red and purest
white, this shining reaper bears within it a power of going forth and
returning.

name: of Pain
of the Eorlingas
desc:
"Forth Eorlingas!"  To the field of Celebrant, Eorl the Young came beyond
hope to save beleaguered Gondor, and from his lance fled massive trolls
and dire wolves.

name: 'Turmil'
desc:
Once wielded by the High Priest of Meneltarma, this great mace gleams
coldly as though moonlit, and can strike equally mighty spiritual and
physical blows.

name: of Amrod
desc:
One of a pair of hunting bows once belonging to Fëanor's two youngest
sons.  The bows are like and yet unlike, much as the twin brothers were.

name: of Amras
desc:
One of a pair of hunting bows once belonging to Fëanor's two youngest
sons.  The bows are like and yet unlike, much as the twin brothers were.

name: 'Belthronding'
desc:
The great bow of Beleg Cúthalion, the most famous archer of the Elves.
Its backbone of black yew holds strings of bear sinew, and of old, only
Beleg himself could draw them.

name: of Bard
desc:
The great yew bow of grim-faced Bard, who shot the mightiest arrow that
songs record.

name: 'Cubragol'
desc:
A crossbow that grants fiery speed to its finder, and imbues its bolts
with flame unquenchable.

name: of Umbar
desc:
A great brazen arbalest with arms of gleaming steel.  It shoots quarrels
with mighty power for those brave enough to risk betrayal.

name: of Erebor
desc:
A fine dwarven pick, good for a miner, and suitable as a weapon
should the wielder ever tunnel through into a foe's stronghold.

name: of Náin
desc:
A mighty Dwarven mattock, as good for hewing orc-necks as for hewing
stone.  Náin, King of the Iron Hills, brought his mattock-wielding
warriors to the Battle of Azanulbizar, and no Orc could withstand them,
although Náin himself fell before their lord Azog.

name: of Fëanor
desc:
This wondrous pair of leather boots once sped Fëanor, creator of the
Silmarils and the mightiest of the Eldar, to fulfill his hero's challenge
and do battle for a Middle-Earth held in thrall.  "Dear-bought those songs
shall be accounted, and yet shall be well-bought.  For the price could be
no other."

name: 'Dal-i-thalion'
desc:
A pair of high-laced shoes, bestowing extraordinary agility and proof
against the powers of corruption and withering.

name: of Wormtongue
desc:
A pair of running shoes once used by the treacherous Gríma son of
Gálmód, the Wormtongue of Edoras.  They are aptly suited for a
messenger, a servant, a spy... or a coward.

name: of Radagast
desc:
Made of brown leather, covered with scuff marks and dust, still these
boots possess the magic of Radagast, friend of birds and nature.

name: of Thrór
desc:
Sturdy footwear of leather and steel as enduring as the long-suffering
Dwarven king-in-exile who wore them.

name: 'Stormwalker'
desc:
And they hewed off Gelmir's hands and feet, and his head last, within
sight of the Elves, and left him.  By ill chance there stood Gwindor
of Nargothrond, the brother of Gelmir.  Now his wrath was kindled to
madness, and he leapt forth on horseback, and many riders with him;
and they pursued the heralds and slew them, and drove on deep into
the main host.

name: of Thranduil
desc:
The hunting cap of King Thranduil, to whose ears come all the secrets of
his forest domain.

name: of Thengel
desc:
A ridged helmet made of steel and embossed with scenes of valor in fine-
engraved silver.  It grants the wearer nobility and understanding.

name: of Celebrimbor
desc:
A metal cap forged by the greatest Noldorin smith of the Second Age,
this helm serves equally well in battle or at the forge, and its
enchantment will never be diminished.

name: of Dor-Lómin
desc:
The legendary Dragon-helm, forged by one of the legendary Dwarven smiths
of the First Age.  The visage of mighty Glaurung forms its crest, and the
mere sight of the helm, borne by Túrin Turambar, struck dread into the
hearts of the servants of Morgoth.

name: 'Holhenneth'
desc:
A famous forged iron helm granting extraordinary powers of mind and
awareness.

name: of Gorlim
desc:
A headpiece, gaudy and barbaric, that betrayed a warrior when he most
needed succor.

name: of Hammerhand
desc:
A great helm, as steady and as distinctive as the hero of the Westdike.
Many long months was Helm Hammerhand besieged in the Hornburg, yet he
strode out time and again among his foes and slew many.  Although they
knew of his coming, none could stand against him.

name: of Berúthiel
desc:
The midnight-hued steel circlet of the sorceress-queen Berúthiel.  It
grants extraordinary powers of sight and awareness at a terrible physical
cost.

name: of Gondor
desc:
From dying Númenor, Elendil brought this shining winged circlet, emblem
of Gondor through an age of the world.

name: of Númenor
desc:
A massive golden crown, set with wondrous jewels of thought and warding,
once worn by the kings of ancient Númenor.

name: 'Hithlomir'
desc:
Familiar with the secret ways hidden in darkness, this leather cuirass is
truly more than it appears.

name: of Himring
desc:
In this studded cuirass of pliable leather lives the memory of
unvanquished Himring, defiant fortress surrounded by the legions of
Morgoth.

name: 'Thalkettoth'
desc:
An amazingly light tunic and skirt sewn with thick, overlapping scales of
hardened leather.  Those who wear it move with agility and assurance.

name: of Arvedui
desc:
A hauberk, leggings, and sleeves of interlocking steel rings, well padded
with leather.  You feel as strong and tall as Arvedui, last king of
Arnor, as you put it on.

name: of Caspanion
desc:
A hauberk, leggings, and sleeves of interlocking steel rings,
strategically reinforced at vital locations with a second layer of chain.
Magics to enhance body and mind lie within, and no door can bar the
wearer's path.

name: of the Rohirrim
desc:
Small steel plates cover an inner layer of sturdy canvas, and both metal
and cloth bear scenes of hunting and war.  As you don the armour of Eorl
the Young, matchless in combat, you feel his spirit surround you.

name: of Isildur
desc:
A gleaming steel suit covering the wearer from neck to foot, with runes of
warding and stability deeply engraved into its surface.

name: 'Belegennon'
desc:
This wondrous shirt of fine-linked chain shimmers before your eyes like
light upon a rippling sea, the gems on it glittering like stars, the
sound of the rings when shaken like the tinkle of rain in a pool.
It stands untouched amidst the fury of the elements, and blows seem
strangely less powerful.

name: of Celeborn
desc:
A shimmering suit of true-silver, forged long ago by dwarven smiths of
legend.  It gleams with purest white as you gaze upon it, and mighty are
its powers to protect and banish.

name: 'Soulkeeper'
desc:
A suit of imperishable adamant with unconquerable strength to endure evil
and disruptive magics.  It protects the life force of its wearer as
nothing else can.

name: 'Colluin'
desc:
A cape worn by a hero from Valinor, a land utterly beyond the strife
of Elements.

name: 'Holcolleth'
desc:
This elven-grey mantle possesses great powers of tranquility and
concealment, and grants the wearer the knowledge and understanding of
the Sindar.

name: of Thingol
desc:
A translucent cloak of pearly grey with glowing, energized Elven runes
showing calm and clear as moonlight on still water.

name: of Thorongil
desc:
This shimmering cloak of greens and browns was once worn by the young
Aragorn, a keen-eyed captain of Rohan and Gondor, valiant on both land
and sea.

name: 'Colannon'
desc:
A crystal-blue cape of fine silk worn by a silent messenger of the forces
of Law.  Seldom will its wearer be diverted from his mission.

name: of Lúthien
desc:
The opaque midnight folds of this cloak, inlaid with a multitude of tiny
diamonds, swirl around you, and you feel a hint, a fragment of the
restorative magics of Lúthien, the most beautiful being who ever knew
death.

name: of Tuor
desc:
The great cloak given to Tuor by the Dweller in the Deep.  As you wrap
yourself from head to foot the words of Ulmo echo: "Thus thou shalt
walk under my shadow".

name: 'Cambeleg'
desc: A hero's handgear that lends great prowess in battle.

name: 'Cammithrim'
desc:
These gloves glow so brightly as to light the way for the wearer and cast
magical bolts with great frequency.

name: 'Paurhach'
desc: A set of gauntlets that smoulder with an unnatural heat.

name: 'Paurnimmen'
desc: A set of handgear, freezing with unnatural cold.

name: 'Pauraegen'
desc: A set of handgear with sparks that crackle across its knuckleguards.

name: 'Paurnen'
desc:
A set of gauntlets that give off a foul, acrid odour yet remain
untarnished.

name: 'Camlost'
desc:
A pair of weakening gauntlets, named after Beren, who returned to Thingol
having lost to Carcharoth the hand that clasped a Silmaril.

name: of Eöl
desc:
The iron-shod gauntlets of the Dark Elven smith Eöl, who imbued them
with magics he could channel in battle.

name: of Fingolfin
desc:
The hand-sheathing of Fingolfin, the Elven warrior-king, who dealt
Morgoth seven mighty wounds and pain that will last until the end of Arda.

name: of Elros
desc:
The shield of Elros Tar-Minyatur, founder of Númenor.  Granted long
life by the Valar, even his shield became imbued with power.

name: of Thorin
desc:
Invoking the strength and endurance of Thorin, King under the Mountain,
this little metal shield is proof against the Element of Earth.

name: of Celegorm
desc:
This shield emblazoned with a multitude of creatures unseen for ages
once protected Celegorm, Lord of Himlad; around it, a mystic balance lies,
containing the conflicts of the elements.

name: of the Haradrim
desc:
The painted shield of a chieftain of Far Harad, gaudy and barbaric.
Its wielder will fear nothing and fight with unnatural strength, but
also draw attention to himself.

name: of Anárion
desc:
The great metal-bound shield of Elendil's younger son Anárion, whom
even Sauron could not corrupt.

name: of Eärendil
desc:
A shining shield wrought for Eärendil Half-Elven, son of Tuor and Idril,
"scored with runes to keep all wounds and harm from him".

name: of Gil-galad
desc:
The legendary shield of Ereinion Gil-galad, who fought his way to the
gates of the Dark Tower, and with whom came light even to Gorgoroth.

name: of Barahir
desc:
Twinned serpents with eyes of emerald meet beneath a crown of flowers to
form this ring, an ancient treasure of Isildur's house.

name: of Tulkas
desc: The treasure of Tulkas, most fleet and wrathful of the Valar.

name: 'Narya'
desc:
The Ring of Fire, made of gold and set with a ruby that glows like flame.
Narya is one of the three Rings of Power created by Celebrimbor and
hidden by the Elves from Sauron.

name: 'Nenya'
desc:
The Ring of Adamant, made of mithril with a pure white stone as
centerpiece.  Nenya is one of the three Rings of Power created by
Celebrimbor and hidden by the Elves from Sauron.

name: 'Vilya'
desc:
The Ring of Sapphire, made of gold with a brilliant blue gem that shines
like stars.  It glitters untouchable despite all that Morgoth ever
wrought.  Vilya is one of the three Rings of Power created by Celebrimbor
and hidden by the Elves from Sauron.

name: 'The One Ring'
desc:
"One Ring to rule them all, One Ring to find them, One Ring to bring
them all and in the darkness bind them."  Made of massive gold, and
inscribed with hidden runes in the foul speech of Mordor, Isildur's Bane
possesses powers so great that it inevitably twists and masters any mortal
being who wears it.

name: of Carlammas
desc: A fiery circle of bronze, with mighty spells to ward off and banish evil.

name: of Ingwë
desc:
The ancient heirloom of Ingwë, high lord of the Vanyar, against whom
nothing of evil could stand.

name: of the Dwarves
desc:
The Nauglamír, a carcanet of gold set with a multitude of shining
gems of Valinor.  The radiant Silmaril of Fëanor hangs in its midst
as its crowning glory.  The sturdy spirits of Dwarvish craftsmen who
labored long in mountain smithies lie within it still, and as gossamer
it rests upon the bearer.

name: 'Elessar'
desc:
A green stone, imbued with the power of Elvendom and the light of the Sun.
Those who gaze through it see the aged and infirm as young again, and
its wearer brings healing after victory in battle.

name: 'Evenstar'
desc:
A plain white jewel, given by Queen Arwen to Frodo Baggins before his
return to the Shire.

name: 'Razorback'
desc:
A massive suit of heavy dragon scales deeply saturated with many colors.
It throbs with angry energies, and you feel the raw elemental might of
untamed Lightning as you put it on.

name: 'Mediator'
desc:
A great suit of dragon hide, set with scales of many hues.  Even the
mightiest wyrms of Law and Chaos fear the judgement of its wearer.

name: 'Bladeturner'
desc:
An adamant suit with scales of every color.  Untrammelled powers elemental
and ethereal swirl around it in an inextricably intermingled nimbus.

name: 'Grond'
desc:
The mighty Hammer of the Underworld, blackened by doomspells of shattering.
Its wielder holds the lives of all Morgoth's servants in his hand.

name: of Morgoth
desc:
Containing much of the power of he who once was mightiest among the Ainur,
this plain iron crown has mounted upon it the two remaining Silmarils,
greatest treasures of Middle-Earth.
